institutes.name: Government Tool Room & Training Centre (GTTC)
institutes.key_courses: CNC Programming & Operation (Turning & Milling), CNC Machine Operator, Tool & Die Maker

institutes.name: RVM CAD
institutes.key_courses: AutoCAD, CNC/VMC setting, manual programming, Mastercam, Delcam

output.rationale: The input provided details for only two institutes (GTTC and RVM CAD), leaving the requested information for three additional institutes missing.

prompt_sent:
You are the Distiller skill. You receive raw text (typically the
`findings` of one or more Researcher nodes, or the `chunks` of a
Retriever node) and produce a small structured record.

You make no tool calls. You do no web access. Everything you need is
already in the prompt under INPUTS.

Procedure:
  1. Identify what fields the user's question implies (people, dates,
     numbers, comparisons, percentages, attributions).
  2. Pull those fields out of the inputs.
  3. Emit a compact JSON record. Fields with no evidence in the inputs
     are omitted, not made up.

Output schema (JSON, no prose, no markdown fences):

  {
    "fields": { "<field_name>": "<value>", ... },
    "rationale": "<one short sentence saying which input supports each field>"
  }

Notes:
  - The fields dictionary is the load-bearing output; downstream
    Formatter nodes read it.
  - When the question is a comparison (`fastest growing`, `largest`),
    emit a `comparison` key with `winner: <id>` and `reason: <short>`.
  - When the question's evidence is missing, set `fields: {}` and put
    the gap in `rationale`. Do not invent.

A Critic node may run after you. Its evaluation will fail if you
invented fields or made claims unsupported by the inputs.

QUESTION: Extract structured details for 5 CNC/VMC training institutes: Name, Key Courses, Duration, and Placement Support.

MEMORY HITS (8 from FAISS):
  - [fact] Population comparison of London, Paris, and Berlin
      source: user_query
      raw: Find the populations of London, Paris, and Berlin and tell me which two are closest in size.
  - [fact] Population comparison of London, Paris, and Berlin
      source: user_query
      raw: Find the populations of London, Paris, and Berlin and tell me which two are closest in size.
  - [fact] Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.
      source: user_query
      raw: Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.
  - [fact] Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.
      source: user_query
      raw: Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.
  - [fact] Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.

  - [fact] Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.

  - [fact] Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes.

INPUTS:
[
  {
    "id": "n:2",
    "kind": "upstream",
    "skill": "researcher",
    "output": {
      "question": "Find 5 reputable CNC/VMC training institutes in Bangalore and provide details on their course offerings, duration, and placement support.",
      "sources": [
        {
          "url": "https://gttc.karnataka.gov.in/4/bangalore-stu-01-stu-02/en",
          "title": "Government Tool Room & Training Centre - Bangalore"
        },
        {
          "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q7JSnrK5GM0",
          "title": "RVM CAD Bangalore CNC/VMC Training"
        }
      ],
      "findings": "The Government Tool Room & Training Centre (GTTC), Bangalore, is a highly reputable government-run institution offering structured courses in CNC and VMC. Their offerings include short-term courses (1 month) such as CNC Programming & Operation (Turning & Milling) and long-term programs (4\u201312 months) like CNC Machine Operator and Tool & Die Maker. Qualifications vary from SSLC to B.E./Diploma depending on the course complexity. GTTC is widely recognized for its extensive training infrastructure and established industry reputation.\n\nRVM CAD (Bangalore centre) is a prominent private training provider specializing in CNC/VMC programming. They offer a 'Skill-30' batch program, which includes training in AutoCAD, CNC/VMC setting, manual programming, and software like Mastercam and Delcam. The duration for these intensive courses is typically 30 days. They explicitly advertise 100% placement support and claim to have a full-scale research lab with 5-axis machines for practical training.\n\nWhile GTTC and RVM CAD are primary examples, many other vocational training centres in Bangalore operate in this domain. However, due to the prevalence of generic aggregator listings and inconsistent data for smaller private institutes, it is highly recommended that candidates verify placement records and hands-on machine availability personally at any centre. For high-reliability training, government-affiliated bodies like GTTC remain the benchmark for curriculum and industry-standard certifications in Karnataka."
    }
  }
]
